title: Reeb Rolls Out Re:Dikyelous US-Made and Hall Pass Taiwan-Made Hardtails
content:
Reeb Cycles celebrates its 15th anniversary with the introduction of the Re:Dikyelous and Hall Pass hardtails. Built around the same aggressive trail philosophy and modern hardtail geometry, the two frames offer riders a choice between a customizable American-made version or a hand built in Taiwan model designed to bring the same ride quality to a broader audience…

Re:Dikyelous and Hall Pass Shared Design

Both the Re:Dikyelous and Hall Pass are designed around Reeb’s modern hardtail platform, prioritizing confident handling, durability, and versatility for aggressive trail riding, long mountain days, and bikepacking adventures.

Shared frame features include:

  • Geometry designed around a 130mm fork
  • 65.5-degree head angle
  • Clearance for up to 2.6” tires
  • Proprietary 3D-printed chainstay yokes for tire clearance and weight savings
  • Portage Dropout System, offering UDH and singlespeed compatibility with up to 15mm of chainstay adjustment
  • 44mm head tube
  • Three water bottle mounts (two inside the front triangle, one under the downtube)
  • External cable routing
  • Dropper post compatibility

While the frames share geometry, features, and intent, they are produced using different manufacturing approaches to meet distinct rider needs.

Re:Dikyelous

The Re:Dikyelous is Reeb’s American-made steel hardtail, hand-built in Longmont, Colorado, featuring US-made Velospec tubing and unlimited color choices. Offered as a tribute to Reeb’s heritage, the Re:Dikyelous reflects the brand’s commitment to small-batch manufacturing, attention to detail, and craftsmanship.

Starting at $2,399.00 (frame only) the 2026 Re:Dikyelous is available in limited sizes now via ReebCycles.com in the United States with additional deliveries beginning late March 2026 in Small, Medium, Large and XL.

Hall Pass

The Hall Pass delivers the same aggressive hardtail performance in a more accessible package. Hand-built in Taiwan using trusted manufacturing partners and available globally, the Hall Pass features a custom-drawn, double-butted steel tubeset, internal ED coating to protect the frame from corrosion and two color choices. Named after Hall Ranch in Lyons, Colorado—where Reeb began testing bikes more than 15 years ago—the Hall Pass represents the brand’s trail-first mindset and commitment to ride quality.

Starting at $1,299 (frame only) the Hall Pass is available for pre-orders in Small, Medium, Large and XL. International orders (frame only) are available for a two-week window (March 4th, 2026 to March 15th, 2026) with early April delivery. US orders are open complete builds and frame only pre-orders with deliveries beginning May 2026.

Check out more at Reeb.

The post Reeb Rolls Out Re:Dikyelous US-Made and Hall Pass Taiwan-Made Hardtails appeared first on The Radavist.
author: John Watson
